title: N-body Gravitational Simulation
source_data_quality_note: Source MD is a widefield-fluorescence fan-out artifact; principle grounded in domain literature.

E.description: N-body Gravitational Simulation: N-body gravitational dynamics: integrate orbits of N point masses under mutual gravitational attraction. The forward operator produces the measurement through a 3-node primitive DAG (F.gravity.nbody…); recovery is posed as a parameter_estimation problem. Difficulty tier delta=3 with effective condition number kappa_eff~500; initial_condition_uncertainty, missing_body_perturbation set the accuracy floor at the Omega boundary. See the forward_model field for the closed-form equation.
E.forward_model: m_i * r_i'' = sum_{j!=i} G m_i m_j (r_j - r_i) / |r_j - r_i|^3
E.observation_y: sky-projected positions and radial velocities (astrometry + spectroscopy)

W.regime: Existence of the recovered 6N_phase_space is guaranteed within the declared Omega bounds. Uniqueness holds on the measurement-supported subspace; out-of-support modes are controlled by declared priors. Stability is conditionally stable (kappa_eff ~= 500); initial_condition_uncertainty dominates the stability cliff; the remaining mismatch parameters contribute higher-order bias terms. Observation gaussian sets the irreducible data-fidelity floor.

C.error_bound: Data-fidelity bound: energy error dE/E0 <= epsilon_step ** 2 per orbit. Noise floor: O(sigma / sqrt(kappa_eff)) with kappa_eff~500. Mismatch-induced bias + C2 * |initial_condition_uncertainty|; iterative solvers achieve q=2 convergence (O(1/k^2)) near the optimum; epsilon_fn captures quality degradation across declared Omega.
C.complexity: O(N ** 2) direct, O(N log N) Barnes-Hut per timestep per iteration
